LAKE WAIKAREITI WALK
trails.description: The Lake Waikareiti Walk will take you two hours to complete a return trip. Along the way you may hear the screech of the kākā  and the chatty ki-ki-ki of the yellow-crowned parakeet (kākāriki).

Waikare Whenua Jnct to Takarua hut
Gray's Bush Scenic Reserve walks
trails.description: These walks pass through a remnant of the tall kahikatea (podocarp) forest which once covered much of the Gisborne plains - the shorter option is suitable for buggies and wheelchairs.

trails.name: Lake Kaitawa Fairy Springs Track
trails.description: This one hour walk will lead you around Lake Kaitawa, a small lake to the south of Lake Waikaremoana. Along the way take the track to Green Lake and Fairy Springs.

trails.name: Lake Waikaremoana: Panekire Bluffs
trails.description: Climb up to Panekire Bluffs for magnificant views of Lake Waikaremoana and the surrounding mountains.

trails.name: Little Manganuku Track
trails.description: This track starts from a historic 'Howe truss' bridge and has excellent waterfall and forest views.

trails.name: Mangakawa Track
trails.description: Follow the Mangakawa Stream with views of waterfalls and small gorges.

trails.name: Manganuku Track
trails.description: This track follows the Manganuku Stream to the Manganuku Hut.

trails.name: Mangaone Caves Track
trails.description: Mangaone Caves are open access and have limestone features like stalactites, stalagmites and calcite wall coverings.

trails.name: Mangapumarumaru Track
trails.description: This strenuous 4 hour track starts in the Waioweka Gorge, 23km south of Ōpōtiki.

trails.name: Marawaiwai Track
trails.description: Marawaiwai Track runs alongside a meandering stream through kahikitea forest. It is suitable for all the family.

trails.name: Moanui, Koranga, Tawa Track
trails.description: This loop track provides options for an overnight tramp with children or a multi-day tramp.

trails.name: Ngamoko Track
trails.description: Walk along the Ngamoko Track as it steadily climbs to the Ngāmoko Range summit. You will be rewarded for your hard work by the views at the summit.

trails.name: Ngutuoha Nature Trail
trails.description: An easy loop trail with interpretation panels provide a 1 hour walking experience in the Waimana Valley at the north end of Te Urewera.

trails.name: Nikau Flat and Kotepato Tracks
trails.description: These two scenic tracks offer excellent opportunity for trout fishing and hunting. The Nikau Flat Track follows alongside the Waioweka River and the Kotepato Track follows alongside the Te Pato Stream.

trails.name: Old Māori Trail
trails.description: The Old Māori Trail is also know as the Rosie Bay - Lake Kaitawa Track and follows what was once a Māori trail to the lake.

trails.name: Onepoto Caves Track
trails.description: The 2 hour return walk to Onepoto Caves branches off to various caves in the area. The track then descends into a small valley where you’ll find the largest number of caves on the walk.

trails.name: Otoko Walkway
trails.description: The Otoko Walkway is 50 km north-west of Gisborne. It traverses 5 km of the old Gisborne/Moutohora railway line. This track is not maintained.

trails.name: Pakihi Track
trails.description: The Pakihi Track is a stunning but challenging 20 km journey through lush forest for trampers and mountain bikers.

trails.name: Ridge Track
trails.description: A demanding track suitable for fit walkers. The tramp will take you 2 hours and leads between the Nikau Loop and Mangakawa tracks.

trails.name: Ruapani Circuit Track
trails.description: It will take you six hours of moderate to hard walking to complete the Ruapani Circuit. The track is accessed from the Waikareiti Track and passes through beautiful forest and seven wetlands.

trails.name: Te Waiti Nature Trail
trails.description: This track is closed and not maintained.

trails.name: Te Waiti Route
trails.description: This route leads through the middle of the Urutawa Conservation Area. As it is rough and gorgy in places it is recommended for experienced trampers only.

trails.name: Nikau Loop Track
trails.description: Discover one of the east coast's last remaining tracts of native coastal forest with its many nikau palms - it's a short, easy walk that's great for kids.

trails.name: Te U Route
trails.description: Te U Route travels from the Waiotahi Valley Road end to Te U Bivvy (staff accommodation) in the Waioweka Conservation Area.

trails.name: Tauranga Loop Track
trails.description: This walking track crosses the historic Tauranga Bridge and passes alongside the Tauranga Stream.

trails.name: Whinray Scenic Reserve Track
trails.description: View the spectacular Motu Falls from the car park and picnic area, or walk along an old coach road through native forest - suitable for all ages.
